authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR administers HIPAA Privacy Rule guidance, making this source authoritative for PHI access, disclosure, and minimum-necessary handling when hospitality operations touches patient or member records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy-sensitive intake, communication, evidence packaging, and patient/member record handling

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Managed Care Manual Chapter 4 - Benefits and Beneficiary Protections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS publishes Medicare managed-care operating guidance, including coverage, organization-determination, referral, authorization, and beneficiary-protection concepts that constrain patient/member hospitality and travel support when payer prerequisites matter.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: coverage, benefits, eligibility, authorization, referral, and payer-sensitive execution gate review

authoritative_sources.title: Hospital Discharge Planning Interpretive Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS survey guidance interprets hospital discharge-planning conditions of participation, including medical-record documentation, patient preferences, transfer/referral obligations, and initial implementation responsibilities that can gate operational travel, lodging, transportation, or care-transition support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: care-transition dependency validation, discharge-linked readiness, transfer/referral evidence handling, and clinical-boundary escalation
used_for: patient preference and referral evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: FHIR Workflow
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 is the recognized healthcare interoperability standards body. FHIR workflow defines request, event, task, encounter, coverage, eligibility, claim, and document-reference patterns that support vendor-neutral lifecycle and evidence terminology for healthcare-administrative work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lifecycle-state modeling, task progression, request/event evidence mapping, and record-of-truth normalization
used_for: healthcare request and event artifact mapping
claim, coverage, encounter, document, and task traceability

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal civil-rights authority for ADA Title III enforcement, and the lodging-reservation provisions govern accessible-room descriptions, holding, blocking, and accommodation-sensitive hospitality readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessible lodging readiness, accommodation-sensitive reservation handling, and guest communication exception routing

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official guidance covers short-term lodging, hotels, vacation rentals, platforms, and travel agents, making it directly relevant to fee, rate, and customer-communication boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fee-sensitive booking, folio, customer-communication, refund-boundary, and service-recovery exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: Fly Rights: A Consumer Guide to Air Travel
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOT is the U.S. aviation consumer-protection authority, and this official guide defines booking details, flight details, complaint routing, and travel-agency service issue handling used for traveler-impact exceptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traveler readiness, itinerary exception, customer-impact complaint, and travel-agency routing

authoritative_sources.title: What Airline Passengers Need to Know About DOT's Automatic Refund Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOT's official refund guidance constrains cancellation, significant-change, delayed-baggage, ancillary-service, and ticket-agent refund boundaries, which prevents this lane from approving refund-sensitive travel exceptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: refund-sensitive itinerary exception, significant-change, ancillary-service issue, and ticket-agent boundary routing

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Security Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized standards bodies, professional frameworks, or public operating references directly relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body, and its standards constrain payment-card handling, token evidence, folio guarantees, and cardholder-data exposure in hospitality and travel operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment guarantee, folio handling, card-token evidence, and payment-data disclosure control

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Operational work is gated by tenant policy, reservation or booking validity, room or service readiness, payment guarantee, requester authority, privacy/consent posture, eligibility, referral, prior authorization, discharge or care-transition documentation, payer or plan requirements, and source-of-truth availability.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: EHR/EMR, clinical documentation, case-management, utilization-review, patient/member access, payer portal, practice-management, revenue-cycle, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, and authorized communication systems establish the healthcare truth set. PMS, CRS, booking, folio, housekeeping, transport, CRM, and guest-communications systems establish the hospitality/travel operational truth set.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops when the answer depends on diagnosis, treatment planning, discharge suitability, medical necessity, coverage interpretation, authorization approval, payer-policy interpretation, privacy-law signoff, minimum-necessary exception approval, clinical risk, or patient-safety clearance.

workflow_stages.name: Accepted work classification and prerequisite gate
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request, identify tenant, property, facility, booking, payer, provider, patient/member, privacy, and source-context prerequisites, and determine whether work can enter execution.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry

workflow_stages.name: Hospitality and healthcare record-of-truth normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize PMS, CRS, booking, room-status, folio, transport, CRM, EHR/EMR, payer portal, case-management, authorization, claim, and care-transition records into one evidence map before lifecycle state changes.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Readiness, execution, hold, and rework progression
workflow_stages.description: Advance or hold work based on reservation readiness, room or service status, transport timing, guest/customer communication, eligibility, referral, authorization, discharge, and consent evidence.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue handling and adjacent-owner routing
workflow_stages.description: Route PHI, patient-safety, licensed clinical, payer-policy, refund, pricing, fraud, payment, accessibility, customer-impact, and legal exceptions to the correct owner without improvising authority.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized standards bodies, professional frameworks, or public operating references directly relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture and reversible closure
workflow_stages.description: Confirm closure only when operational actions, records, owner acknowledgements, customer or patient/member communication evidence, and healthcare-administrative dependencies are traceable and residual exceptions are closed or routed.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry

workflow_stages.name: Tenant adaptation and source conflict control
workflow_stages.description: Apply tenant policy, brand standards, payer rules, provider policies, supplier terms, processor constraints, and jurisdictional requirements as overlays while recording assumptions and escalating material conflicts.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Primary regulators, statutes, agency manuals, or official public guidance tied to the named industry

artifacts: reservations, booking confirmations, PNRs, itineraries, CRS records, PMS room status, housekeeping boards, folios, payment guarantees, transport records, incident logs, CRM records, guest profiles, and communication logs
eligibility, benefits, referral, prior authorization, organization-determination, member status, service date, provider, facility, plan, and payer portal records
EHR/EMR encounter notes, orders, discharge or care-transition records, clinical-administrative notes, consent records, patient/member access notes, and case-management tasks
charge capture, coding references, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, reimbursement, and revenue-cycle documentation when they affect operational closure or routing
tenant policies, brand standards, approval matrices, service models, owner maps, privacy/minimum-necessary rules, record-location references, and retrieval hooks
exception notes, rework logs, completion evidence records, escalation packages, source conflict notes, and human review decisions
systems_of_record: payment gateway, card-token, folio, or revenue ledger
EHR or EMR and clinical documentation systems
case management and utilization review systems
practice management, revenue cycle, and payer portal systems
patient/member access and authorized communication systems
claim, remittance, denial, and appeal systems
privacy, compliance, incident, fraud, and safety/security case systems

allowed: Classify hospitality operations requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, exception-sensitive, or owned by another lane.
Request the minimum tenant facts, source context, systems of record, and authoritative records needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, escalate, fulfill, or close work based on documented lifecycle state and evidence.
Prepare execution status updates, operational readiness summaries, exception queue notes, completion evidence records, and adjacent-owner escalation notes.
Validate whether required records exist and are traceable without interpreting clinical, payer, legal, financial, privacy, or safety authority.
escalate: Eligibility, referral, authorization, discharge, consent, service-date, patient/member, claim, booking, PMS, CRS, transport, payment, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
PHI, minimum-necessary, patient-safety, licensed clinical, discharge suitability, medical-necessity, payer-policy, coverage, coding, denial, or appeal interpretation changes the answer.
Refund, rate, fee, compensation, payment reversal, chargeback, fraud, accessibility-law, legal, safety/security, public-health, or executive policy authority is required.
Public baseline and tenant, payer, provider, brand, supplier, processor, jurisdiction, or facility rules materially conflict.
refuse: Mark work complete without closure evidence from authoritative hospitality and healthcare systems of record.
Diagnose, plan treatment, clear travel after discharge, determine medical necessity, interpret payer policy, or approve coverage, authorization, coding, claims, denials, or appeals.
Expose PHI, guest identity data, or full payment-card data through unapproved channels.
Override rates, refunds, fees, compensation, service recovery, fraud, safety, privacy, or legal policies without explicit documented authority.

domain_failure_modes: hospitality work is marked complete without PMS, CRS, folio, communication, EHR, authorization, or case-management evidence
missing eligibility, referral, authorization, or service-date facts are ignored and the patient/member is routed to unsupported lodging or travel
PHI is copied into guest-facing or hotel-facing communication channels without minimum-necessary controls
a discharge, travel, accommodation, or transport decision requiring licensed clinical judgment is treated as routine hospitality execution
payer medical-necessity, coverage, coding, denial, or appeal interpretation is improvised by the hospitality lane
room readiness, transport acceptance, and guest communications conflict across systems but the lifecycle state is advanced anyway
customer pressure causes unauthorized fee waiver, refund, compensation, or payment reversal commitments
public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, brand standard, or jurisdiction-specific requirement conflicts but the lane synthesizes a confident answer
the overlay collapses into generic operations language and omits reservation, PMS/CRS, folio, readiness, EHR/EMR, authorization, claim, discharge, and privacy artifacts

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: The queue label is hospitality-and-travel while the resolved industry profile is healthcare-and-care-delivery.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The package preserves hospitality operations terminology and the required package path while satisfying the repository-resolved healthcare profile. Runtime routing must still retrieve tenant, payer, provider, facility, and property context before applying the lane.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific property, payer, provider, brand, supplier, processor, and jurisdiction rules can be stricter than the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This is handled through required runtime retrieval, source-precedence recording, and escalation when conflict changes the operational answer.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack release.

Research constrained the scenarios toward prerequisite gates, lifecycle reversibility, PHI controls, clinical/payer escalation, hospitality terminology, and completion evidence rather than only happy-path operations.
